content_preview.title: Perplexity Unveils Personal Computer: An Always-On AI Agent Running on Mac Mini
content_preview.summary: Perplexity announces Personal Computer at Ask 2026, turning a Mac mini into a persistent AI agent with local file access, 20 orchestrated models, and enterprise integrations.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Perplexity used its inaugural Ask 2026 developer conference — held inside a former church in San Francisco's North Beach — to announce Personal Computer, a product that turns a Mac mini into an always-on AI agent with local access to a user's files, applications, and sessions. The announcement, [reported by 9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/11/perplexitys-personal-computer-is-a-cloud-based-ai-agent-running-on-mac-mini/), marks a significant expansion for the company, which also u...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article (584 words) with clear sections covering overview, capabilities, pricing, security, enterprise, and open questions. Professional tone throughout. Good use of inline citations.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources verified via committed source snapshots. 9to5Mac confirmed product details and security model. Axios confirmed Srinivas quote verbatim. VentureBeat confirmed enterprise features including SOC 2 Type II (two explicit mentions). TechCrunch confirmed Perplexity Computer context. Digital Trends confirmed $200/month pricing, 10,000 credits, waitlist, Mac-only.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims align with cited sources. The bot's Round 1 rewrite correctly moved the Srinivas quote attribution from VentureBeat to Axios. The bot's dispute of the SOC 2 Type II finding is valid — VentureBeat explicitly states 'SOC 2 Type II certification' and 'SOC 2 Type II compliance' in the article text. The Round 1 reviewer erred on this point.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The 'What We Don't Know' section adds appropriate caveats about privacy implications and unanswered questions.
editor_notes.originality: No existing articles on Perplexity Personal Computer in the archive. Fresh topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review. The bot addressed Finding 1 (quote misattribution) correctly and successfully disputed Finding 2 (SOC 2 Type II) with evidence from the VentureBeat source snapshot. Both corrections verified independently. Human-requested article with heightened scrutiny applied — no framing bias or source independence issues detected. Ready for publication.
